trends.name: Iran Oil Crisis & Strait of Hormuz Disruption
trends.summary: Strait of Hormuz oil flows have collapsed from 19.5M to 0.5M barrels/day following Iran tensions. Trump weighing military strikes on Iran's oil infrastructure while considering ceasefire talks. G7 emergency SPR releases insufficient to offset supply shock, creating massive tailwinds for US oil companies' cash flow (expected to jump from $62B to $163B annually).

trends.name: US-China Trade Summit & Tariff Negotiations
trends.summary: Scott Bessent, Jamieson Greer, and China's Vice Premier He Lifeng began high-level trade talks in Paris to map plans for upcoming leaders' summit. This signals potential de-escalation or major tariff announcements ahead, with direct implications for equity valuations and sector rotation.

trends.name: Fed Rate Outlook Shift & Policy Reversal
trends.summary: Markets anticipating massive shift in Fed's rate outlook, with eight G10 central banks meeting this week. Potential policy reversals could reshape fixed income and equities valuations, particularly affecting dividend stocks and high-yield plays currently attracting yield-chasing investors.

trends.name: Oil Majors & Energy Stock Windfall Profits
trends.summary: Rising oil prices from $55 to $100/barrel creating unprecedented free cash flow for US energy companies ($163B annually vs $62B previously). Investors rotating into dividend-paying energy stocks and MLPs like Energy Transfer while betting on continued geopolitical premium in oil.

trends.name: 2008 Financial Crisis Analog & Market Structure Risk
trends.summary: Market strategists (Hartnett) warning of concerning parallels to 2008 financial crisis setup. Combined with private credit selloff risks and potential bank contagion concerns, suggests systemic risk building in markets despite equity rally, warranting defensive positioning.

trends.name: Private Credit Market Deterioration & Bank Risk
trends.summary: Private credit selloff accelerating with rising contagion risk to banking sector. As rates remain volatile and credit conditions tighten, exposure to illiquid credit instruments poses systemic threat similar to pre-crisis dynamics, affecting levered buyout firms and mid-market lenders.

trends.name: Nvidia GTC 2025: AI Hype Cycle Inflection Point
trends.summary: Nvidia facing unusually high expectations at GTC event amid growing AI commodity competition and valuation pressures. Event outcome could trigger major tech sector repricing and AI enthusiasm moderation if guidance disappoints versus consensus hype.

trends.name: Emerging Markets Rotation & Geopolitical Shift
trends.summary: Investors recalibrating emerging markets exposure amid shifting global power dynamics, Iran-US tensions, and China trade negotiations. Potential reallocation from developed markets into EM opportunities as geopolitical landscape reshapes trade flows and growth expectations.

trends.name: FedEx Earnings & Transportation Cost Inflation
trends.summary: FedEx earnings kick off corporate reporting week with focus on how rising fuel costs from oil price surge impact margins. Results will signal whether energy inflation passes through supply chains or compresses profitability, affecting industrials and logistics sector guidance.

trends.name: Strategic Petroleum Reserve Policy & Election Politics
trends.summary: Trump signaling willingness to reduce SPR releases to support oil prices, reversing Biden-era drawdown strategy. Political incentives to keep gas prices low (and oil profits high for energy sector) creating policy uncertainty, with implications for energy independence and geopolitical leverage.

trends.name: Dividend & Income Yield Plays in Rate Uncertainty
trends.summary: Investors hunting high yields (6-8% strategies, TriplePoint 17% yield, equity fund call-overlays) ahead of potential Fed pivot. As rate direction becomes uncertain, yield-focused equity plays and dividend stocks attracting capital while traditional fixed income offers less appeal.

trends.name: Valuation Reset Opportunities in Tech & Growth
trends.summary: Unity Software and other growth/tech names experiencing valuation resets after previous inflation cycle. Investors identifying rare entry points before institutional mega-funds deploy capital, signaling potential outperformance window in previously beaten-down tech stocks if Fed pauses tightening.
